project.name: OneBasePlate
project.goal: Reduce grocery stress, separate cooking events, takeaway reliance, and food waste in households with overlapping but conflicting food preferences, including ARFID-style preparation constraints, picky children, and babies requiring texture-adapted food.
project.northStar: One base meal, multiple household-specific assemblies, with a merged grocery list and low-decision rescue mode.
primarySuccessMetrics: Reduce number of separate cooking events per day
Increase percentage of dinners assembled from one shared base
Reduce food waste from unused ingredients
Reduce time spent deciding what to cook
nonGoals: Building a massive public recipe marketplace in v1
Advanced nutrition coaching or medical advice
Live grocery delivery integrations in v1
Fully automated AI meal generation without explicit household constraints

anthropicLongRunningAgentAlignment.intent: Structure the repo and work plan so a long-running coding agent can make safe incremental progress across sessions.
principles: Use a dedicated initializer/setup phase before feature implementation begins.
Represent requirements as a structured JSON feature list with pass/fail status rather than freeform notes.
Work on one feature at a time and leave the codebase in a clean, mergeable state after each session.
Persist progress in a progress log plus git history so future sessions can recover context quickly.
Start each session by reading the feature list, recent progress, and repository state.
Run end-to-end verification before marking any feature as passing.
Favor deterministic fixtures and explicit acceptance steps over vague goals.
sessionLoop: Run init.sh and verify the app boots successfully.
Read agent-progress.md and recent git log entries.
Read PRD.json and select the highest-priority feature with passes=false and no blocked dependencies.
Implement only that feature and any strictly necessary enabling refactor.
Run unit tests and end-to-end checks relevant to the feature.
Update only the affected passes fields and append a short progress summary.
Commit the work with a descriptive message.

personas.name: Constraint-led adult eater
personas.description: Adult with ARFID-like or highly specific preparation constraints.
needs: Food prepared in an acceptable way

personas.name: Picky toddler
personas.description: Young child with a small set of accepted foods and fast-changing tolerance.
needs: At least one safe food on the plate

personas.name: Weaning baby
personas.description: Baby requiring soft, mashable, or finger-safe texture adaptations.
needs: Simple substitutions when base meal is unsuitable

personas.name: Busy home-working adult
personas.description: Adult with limited time who wants low-decision household planning.
needs: Predictable system that reduces mental load

coreConcepts.baseMeal: A flexible meal made of components that can be assembled differently for each household member.
coreConcepts.assemblyVariant: A per-person presentation of the base meal that respects preferences, preparation constraints, and texture needs.
coreConcepts.safeFood: A reliably accepted ingredient or meal component for a person.
coreConcepts.rescueMeal: A low-effort meal generated for high-stress or low-time situations using pantry/freezer staples first.
coreConcepts.overlapScore: A score showing how many household members can eat a given ingredient or base meal with acceptable modifications.

suggestedStack.storage: Local-first JSON or IndexedDB/localStorage for v1

architectureNotes: Prefer local-first storage for rapid MVP delivery and privacy.
Keep the rule engine explicit and deterministic before adding AI generation.
Ship fixtures for 3-5 representative households to support repeatable development and testing.
Treat the PRD feature list as the source of truth for incremental implementation.

features.description: Repository initializer creates bootstrap artifacts for long-running agent work
steps: Create project scaffold and package metadata
Add init.sh to install dependencies and start the local app
Add agent-progress.md with initial status and conventions
Add PRD.json to repo root as the structured source of truth
Add sample fixtures for at least one representative household

features.description: User can create a household with multiple members and role types
steps: Navigate to household setup
Add at least four members with adult, toddler, and baby roles
Save the household profile
Re-open the household
Verify all members persist correctly

features.description: User can record safe foods, hard-no foods, and preparation rules for each household member
steps: Open a member profile
Add safe foods, hard-no foods, and preparation rules
Save the profile
Re-open the profile
Verify all constraints are preserved exactly

features.description: User can define pantry, fridge, freezer, and staple ingredients
steps: Navigate to household ingredients
Add ingredients across pantry, fridge, and freezer categories
Tag ingredients with properties such as quick, mashable, or rescue
Save the ingredient set
Verify ingredients are available to the planner

features.description: User can create or select a base meal made from reusable components
steps: Navigate to base meals
Create a meal from components such as protein, carb, and veg
Set time and rescue eligibility metadata
Save the base meal
Verify it appears in meal selection

features.description: Planner generates one base meal with per-person assembly variants
steps: Open the planner for a household with saved constraints
Select or generate a base meal
View assembly instructions for each member
Verify each member receives a compatible version or an explicit fallback
Verify the plan clearly identifies shared versus extra preparation

features.description: Planner adapts baby servings into texture-safe versions for a no-teeth weaning stage
steps: Open a generated meal that includes a baby household member
Inspect the baby assembly instructions
Verify the output transforms unsuitable textures into soft, mashable, or finger-safe guidance
Verify obviously unsafe suggestions are excluded
Verify the baby variant stays tied to the same base meal where possible

features.description: Planner ensures toddler plate includes at least one safe food
steps: Generate a meal for a household with a toddler profile
Inspect the toddler assembly variant
Verify at least one marked safe food is included
If no direct fit exists, verify an explicit fallback safe food is suggested
Verify the output never silently omits toddler compatibility

features.description: Planner computes overlap score for ingredients and meals
steps: Open ingredient or meal details
View overlap score and compatibility summary
Verify score reflects how many household members can eat it directly or with acceptable adaptation
Verify members with hard-no conflicts are clearly identified
Verify overlap score is used in meal ranking

features.description: User can generate a low-decision weekly plan from available meals and ingredients
steps: Open weekly planning
Generate a five- or seven-day plan
Verify each day contains a base meal and per-person variants
Verify repeated ingredients are reused intentionally across the week
Verify the plan can be saved and re-opened

features.description: App produces one merged grocery list from the weekly plan
steps: Generate and save a weekly plan
Open the grocery list
Verify repeated ingredients are consolidated
Verify pantry or already-owned items can be excluded
Verify the list is readable and grouped by category

features.description: Rescue mode generates the fastest acceptable meal using household staples first
steps: Open rescue mode
Choose a scenario such as low-energy, low-time, or everyone-melting-down
Generate a rescue meal
Verify the output prioritizes freezer or pantry staples when available
Verify the output still includes per-person assemblies

features.description: User can pin household-approved meals into a rotation
steps: Generate or open a meal that worked well
Pin it to the household rotation
Open the rotation view
Verify pinned meals are saved and reusable
Verify future planning can prioritize pinned meals

features.description: User can mark meals as success, partial success, or failure and capture quick notes
steps: Open a planned meal after serving
Record an outcome and optional notes
Save the result
Re-open meal history
Verify the outcome is persisted and visible

features.description: Planner learns from meal outcomes to rank reliable meals higher
steps: Record several successful and failed meal outcomes
Generate a new plan
Verify meals with stronger success history are ranked higher when constraints are similar
Verify hard-no or repeated-failure meals are deprioritized
Verify ranking remains explainable to the user

features.description: App loads quickly and supports same-session editing without noticeable lag for a typical household
steps: Launch the app with a representative fixture household
Navigate across setup, planning, and grocery screens
Edit member constraints and regenerate a plan
Verify interactions feel immediate for local MVP use
Verify no screen hangs or crashes during normal flows

features.description: All critical planning flows are covered by deterministic fixtures and end-to-end tests
steps: Run the automated test suite
Verify household setup flow is covered
Verify base meal planning flow is covered
Verify rescue mode is covered
Verify grocery list generation is covered

features.description: Planner explains why a meal was recommended and what the trade-offs are
steps: Generate a meal recommendation
Inspect the explanation panel
Verify the app states why the meal fits the household
Verify any extra prep or fallback requirements are surfaced
Verify the explanation is brief and understandable

features.description: Planner allows quick editing of a single member without redoing the whole household setup
steps: Open an existing household
Edit one member's constraints
Save changes
Regenerate the meal plan
Verify only the relevant compatibility results change

features.description: User can export or print the weekly plan and grocery list
steps: Open a saved weekly plan
Choose export or print
Verify the plan includes daily meals, per-person variants, and grocery list
Verify formatting is readable
Verify exported output matches the saved plan

fixtureHouseholds.name: Conflicting household baseline
fixtureHouseholds.description: One adult with strict preparation constraints, one picky toddler, one no-teeth weaning baby, and one adult with unrelated preferences.

fixtureHouseholds.name: Two adults plus toddler
fixtureHouseholds.description: Simpler fixture used to prove the planner still works when no baby adaptations are needed.

fixtureHouseholds.name: Single caregiver plus two children
fixtureHouseholds.description: Used to test rescue mode and low-time planning flows.
